arts.ttl: What I don't want to see from Stoke City in final five games
arts.sum: We can get the game going from 60 yards closer to the goal we’re trying to score in, as a home team pressing in the attacking half. You can spend your energy there rather than putting yourselves under pressure in your defensive third, asking players who are in the team to defend and win the ball to try to play around athletic and willing forwards.

arts.ttl: Stoke City reveal steer for new badge design
arts.sum: More than 5,000 Stoke City supporters have joined in consultation about potential changes to the club badge – and given a clear steer about what they want and don’t want in any new design. A total 81 per cent of fans who took part in initial surveys said they were open to the idea of a new badge in the first major review of the crest that was introduced back in 2001.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins falling in love with Stoke City, bones to pick and how he's staying calm
arts.sum: There are “bones to be picked out” from the season as a whole after whatever is to happen has played out by full-time on May 3 but for now, all focus is on a huge run of games which will dictate Stoke’s fate in the division. Robins said: “I think there are good players, there really are.

arts.ttl: Pundit makes prediction for 'huge' Stoke City clash with Cardiff amid Tony Pulis confession
arts.sum: Former Cardiff striker Blake is on duty with Radio Stoke this weekend for a clash between two teams separated by just two points at the bottom of the Championship with only four games remaining after this one. The winner of Stoke City’s six-pointer with Cardiff will be the team that shows the most aggression, pundit Nathan Blake predicts.
